name: Taiwan
overview: Taiwan's Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) applies to both public and private sectors. A 2023 amendment raised security-breach fines and mandated creation of an independent supervisory authority; the Personal Data Protection Commission (PDPC) was formally established in 2025, replacing fragmented ministry-by-ministry enforcement. Further amendment work is ongoing.

legal_instruments.title: Personal Data Protection Act
legal_instruments.summary: Comprehensive law on collection, processing and use of personal data with notification duties, consent requirements, sensitive-data restrictions, and civil, administrative and criminal liability. 2023 amendment increased fines for security failures to NTD 15 million.

legal_instruments.title: Enforcement Rules of the Personal Data Protection Act
legal_instruments.summary: Implementing rules defining key terms (e.g. appropriate security measures), specifying notification methods and data-subject request handling.

developments.title: Personal Data Protection Commission established
developments.summary: Taiwan's independent PDPC formally commenced operations as the dedicated data protection authority, consolidating enforcement previously spread across sector ministries.

developments.title: PDPA amendment raises fines and mandates independent DPA
developments.summary: Amendment increased penalties for inadequate security measures (up to NTD 15 million for serious violations) and required establishment of an independent supervisory authority.

penalties: Administrative fines generally up to NTD 15 million for serious security-measure violations (2023 amendment); criminal liability up to 5 years' imprisonment for unlawful use with intent to profit.
cross_border: Transfers may be restricted by the competent authority where national interests are affected, an international treaty requires it, the receiving country lacks sound protection, or the transfer circumvents the Act.
